news.summary: A fitness brand specializing in infrared sauna workouts has recently inaugurated its initial studio in Dubai as part of its global expansion strategy. The brand, HOTWORX, is also commemorating the eighth anniversary of its flagship location in Oxford. To enhance customer experience, the fitness franchise has introduced a "Scan & Go" retail feature and innovative equipment named "Gluteus Max". Originating from New Orleans, these developments mark significant milestones for the brand, which is gaining traction internationally.
news.headline: HOTWORX® opens first Dubai studio, celebrates 8-year Oxford location, introduces new equipment and retail feature.

news.summary: In the 9th annual MedTech Breakthrough Awards, Vori Health, the sole nationwide physician-led medical practice specializing in musculoskeletal (MSK) care, has been acclaimed as the "Best Patient Experience Solution Provider". This accolade celebrates Vori's exceptional services and dedication to enhancing patient experience. This recognition further solidifies Vori Health's position as a leader in the MSK care field.
news.headline: Vori Health wins "Best Patient Experience Solution" award at MedTech Breakthrough Awards 2025. #MSKCare #MedTechBreakthrough

news.summary: Coinbase Global, Inc. (NASDAQ: COIN) is set to announce its Q1 earnings after the market closes on Thursday, May 8. Analysts are predicting earnings per share of $2.11 for the New York-based company. Revenue for the quarter is projected to be around $2.12 billion, marking an increase from $1.64 billion in the previous year, as reported by Benzinga Pro. In the last quarter, Coinbase surpassed revenue expectations and has exceeded estimates in eight of the last ten quarters. The company's shares dipped by 0.2% on Wednesday and closed at $196.56. For more details, Benzinga readers can view the most recent analyst ratings on the Analyst Stock Ratings page, where they can filter results by stock ticker, company name, analyst firm, rating change, and more.
news.headline: Coinbase (NASDAQ: COIN) to release Q1 earnings with projections: $2.11 per share and $2.12 billion revenue, shares down 0.2%.

news.summary: As of 2025, smart contracts are gaining relevance in the finance sector with billions flowing through decentralized protocols and institutional players getting involved in Web3. Smart contracts are self-executing programs that run without downtime, fraud, or interference, automatically executing transactions when conditions are met without the need for intermediaries. Initially popularized by Ethereum, smart contracts now exist across multiple chains like Solana, Avalanche, Cardano, Polkadot, and layer-2s like Arbitrum. Their promise lies in efficiency, transparency, and automation of processes like decentralized exchanges, lending protocols, NFT marketplaces, and insurance payouts. Understanding their strengths, weaknesses, and evolution is crucial for investors making informed bets on the future of finance.
news.headline: Smart contracts' readiness for mainstream questioned as billions flow through protocols. Ethereum popularized, now on multiple chains. Investors advised to understand their success and challenges. Read more on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: LACFO New York, a contemporary fragrance company focused on enhancing everyday experiences through immersive sensory experiences, has recently expanded its partnership with Listrak to support its rapid growth in the fast-paced fragrance industry. This expansion aims to provide scalability for LACFO as it continues its upward trajectory. The partnership was solidified on May 8, 2025, in Lititz, Pennsylvania.
news.headline: LAFCO expands partnership with Listrak to fuel personalized customer communications, supporting rapid growth in fragrance category.

news.summary: The Management Board of Northern Horizon Capital AS has given the green light to the unaudited financial figures of the Baltic Horizon Fund for the first quarter of 2025.
news.headline: Q1 2025 unaudited results of Baltic Horizon Fund approved by Northern Horizon Capital AS board.

news.summary: The Victory Bank in Horsham, PA is marking the launch of its second retail branch at 100 Gibraltar Road with a week-long celebration from June 2 to June 6, 2025. The event will include daily prize distributions, family-friendly activities, business seminars, and special deals. Among these offers is a CD Special boasting an annual percentage yield (APY) of 4.5% for 22 weeks. Additionally, unique promotions will be available for new business accounts established in person at the Horsham branch.
news.headline: The Victory Bank's new Horsham branch's grand opening event offers a CD Special of 4.5% APY for 22 weeks and a chance to win a trip to Maui.

news.summary: A Launch Party is set to take place on May 15 in Mankato, Minnesota to introduce a new treatment option. The therapy is designed to provide relief for those experiencing depression, stress, or mental fog without the need for medication. Between the Bridges Healing Center in Mankato is offering this innovative treatment for individuals seeking alternative approaches to their mental health. The event is scheduled for May 15 and aims to showcase the benefits of this medication-free option for individuals in the area dealing with mental health issues.
news.headline: Between the Bridges Healing Center in Mankato offers brain stimulation to treat mental health issues, with a launch party on May 15.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, in Chicago, a leading travel insurance company, AXA Partner US, unveiled its latest line of Schengen travel insurance policies tailored for Americans. These plans aim to cater to the various requirements of travelers heading to Europe, with coverage including medical expenses, trip cancellations, and personal belongings. This launch showcases AXA's commitment to serving the US market and providing comprehensive insurance options for individuals embarking on trips to Schengen Area countries.
news.headline: AXA Partners US introduces new Schengen travel insurance plans for US residents, tailored to diverse needs.

news.summary: A new report by Grand View Research, Inc. predicts that the global bottled water market will grow significantly, with an estimated size of USD 509.18 billion by 2030. This growth is attributed to a projected Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 6.4% from 2025 to 2030. The demand for bottled water is the key driving factor behind this market expansion.
news.headline: Bottled water market to hit $509.18 Billion by 2030, growing at 6.4% CAGR - Grand View Research.

news.summary: In a joint effort, Cellipont Bioservices, a prominent cell therapy Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO), and Optieum Biotechnologies from EHIME, Japan are collaborating to utilize the OPTF01 product to advance the development of the next-generation CAR-T therapy. The collaboration, announced on May 8, 2025, at THE WOODLANDS, Texas aims to push forth the innovative CAR-T technology for cell therapies. This partnership signifies a significant milestone in the field, leveraging cutting-edge solutions to propel the advancement of cell-based therapies.
news.headline: Cellipont Bioservices and Optieum Biotechnologies partner for CAR-T therapy for Glioblastoma; Collaboration announced on May 8, 2025.

news.summary: Papa John's International, Inc. saw its shares (NASDAQ: PZZA) rise in premarket trading. The company posted adjusted first-quarter earnings per share of 36 cents, surpassing the analyst estimate of 35 cents. Quarterly sales reached $518.31 million, a 1% increase compared to the previous year, exceeding the Street's expectation of $513.55 million. The rise in revenues was driven by a $11.4 million surge in Commissary revenues, influenced by higher commodity prices during the quarter. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Papa John's beats Q1 estimates with 36 cents EPS on sales of $518.31 million, up 1% YoY. Commissary revenues drive growth.

news.summary: Johnson Controls International plc (NYSE: JCI) exceeded expectations with its second-quarter earnings report. The company's sales for the quarter grew by 1.4% year over year to $5.676 billion, surpassing the estimated $5.63 billion. Their adjusted earnings per share (EPS) also saw a significant increase from 69 cents to 82 cents year over year, beating the consensus estimate of 59 cents. Looking ahead to the third quarter, Johnson Controls anticipates an adjusted EPS of $0.97 to $1.00, slightly below the estimated $1.00. The company foresees mid-single digit organic sales growth and aims for an Adjusted segment EBITA margin of around 17.5% for Q3. Furthermore, Johnson Controls raised its full-year 2025 adjusted EPS outlook from $3.50-$3.60 to $3.60.
news.headline: Johnson Controls (NYSE: JCI) reports Q2 sales of $5.676B, beating estimates. Raised FY25 adjusted EPS outlook to $3.60.

news.summary: NHC has made a significant leap, climbing 70 spots to reach the 78th position on the esteemed Builder 100 List. This rise was driven by a remarkable 145% surge in home closings. The corporation has attributed its success to its dedication to offering affordable, quality homes, with prices commencing at $180,000. This achievement was announced in Houston on May 8, 2025 through a press release from PRNewswire.
news.headline: NHC rises to #78 on Builder 100 List after 70-spot leap, with a 145% home closings increase and affordable, quality homes starting at $180s.

news.summary: In Pittsburgh on May 8, 2025, Koppers Holdings Inc. (NYSE: KOP), a company offering treated wood products, wood treatment chemicals, and carbon compounds worldwide, declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 per share.
news.headline: Koppers Holdings Inc. (NYSE: KOP) declares quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 per share for shareholders.

news.summary: In Bangalore, India, a report released on May 8, 2025, indicates that the Microphysiological System Market is divided into three main segments based on type, which are Human Organ and Tissue Models, Disease Models, and Non-Human Species Models. These systems serve various applications, including Pharmaceutical & Biotechnology Companies, and Academic & Research Institutes. The global market for Microphysiological Systems is experiencing considerable growth and is estimated to be valued at a significant figure.
news.headline: Microphysiological System Market to grow from USD 148M in 2024 to USD 1134M by 2031, 34.3% CAGR. Key segments: Human Organ Models, Disease Models, Non-Human Models. Major applications: Pharmaceutical & Biotechnology, Academic & Research.

news.summary: A new manufacturing facility for aerospace coatings and sealants is set to be constructed in Shelby, North Carolina by PPG. The company has announced an investment of $380 million for this project, showcasing their commitment to expanding operations in the United States. This facility is expected to bolster production capacity and create opportunities for growth in the aerospace industry.
news.headline: PPG investing $380M to construct U.S. manufacturing plant in Shelby, N.C. for aerospace products.
Check out the latest data on the Consumer Discretionary sector and industry performance at Bloomberg with insights on key numbers.

news.summary: Takeda Pharmaceutical Company Limited (NYSE: TAK) announced its fiscal year 2024 financial results, with a net profit of 107.9 billion yen (approximately $709.86 million), marking a 25.1% decrease compared to the previous year. The core net profit saw a 2.5% increase to 775.6 billion yen, which, at a constant currency, was a decrease of 3.4%. CEO Christophe Weber expressed satisfaction, stating that Takeda had achieved excellent results in FY2024. The company's revenue stood at 4.26 trillion yen (around $28.4 billion), marking a 7.5% increase (or 29% increase at constant currency rates). This growth was driven by favorable foreign exchange rates and the success in key areas such as Gastroenterology (GI), Rare Diseases, and Plasma-Derived Therapies (PDT). Weber also highlighted the positive impact of their efficiency program in delivering significant cost savings.
news.headline: Takeda reports Fiscal Year 2024 net profit of 107.9 billion yen ($709.86 million), down 25.1% YoY, but core net profit up 2.5% to 775.6 billion yen. Revenue increased to 4.26 trillion yen ($28.4 billion). CEO pleased with performance.

news.summary: Shares of Crocs Inc. (NASDAQ: CROX) surged in premarket trading following the release of their first-quarter earnings report. The company disclosed a 0.14% year-on-year drop in first-quarter FY25 revenue to $937.33 million, surpassing the analyst consensus of $907.89 million. Their adjusted EPS came in at $3.00, outperforming the expected $2.48. Crocs brand revenues saw a 2.4% increase to $762 million (or 4.2% in constant currency terms). Direct-to-consumer revenues climbed 1.1% to $285 million, while wholesale revenues rose by 3.2% to $477 million. On the other hand, HEYDUDE's total revenues declined by 9.8% to $176 million, with direct-to-consumer revenues growing by 8.3% to $65 million and wholesale revenues dropping by 17.9% to $111 million.
news.headline: Crocs Inc. stock surges as first-quarter revenue declines by 0.14% to $937.33 million, beating estimates. EPS reaches $3.00. Brand and direct-to-consumer revenues see growth. Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Arm Holdings PLC – ADR (NASDAQ: ARM) announced its fourth-quarter earnings exceeding expectations, despite issuing a fiscal year 2026 guidance below projections. The company reported earnings of 55 cents per share for the quarter, surpassing the analyst consensus of 52 cents. Quarterly revenue stood at $1.24 billion, also beating the estimated $1.23 billion. Arm highlighted record-breaking results for Q4 and the full fiscal year ending 2025, achieving over $1 billion in revenue for the first time in Q4. The growth was attributed to increased deployment of CSS platforms. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Arm Holdings PLC reported better-than-expected Q4 earnings, 55 cents/share, $1.24B revenue. Analysts revised forecasts downward.

news.summary: A recent survey by Passport and Drive Research has shown that ecommerce brands are effectively managing increasing trade risks while still tapping into the lucrative international market. The study, conducted in PALO ALTO, Calif. on May 8, 2025, highlighted the strategies employed by these brands to expand their reach smoothly. The research sheds light on the challenges faced by companies as trade risks rise, but also outlines the significant revenue potential available in the international market.
news.headline: 81% of ecommerce leaders fear tariffs, yet 91% see international as key for growth. Survey by Passport and Drive Research.

news.summary: In Riverside, California, the Blue Zones Project Palm Springs is hosting a Kickoff event on May 15, 2025, from 5:30 to 7:30 p.m. at Festival Theaters. All community members are invited to attend this free event, which signifies the official launch of Blue Zones Project Palm Springs, an initiative aimed at promoting healthy living and well-being in the area.
news.headline: Blue Zones Project Palm Springs Kick-Off event on May 15 at Festival Theaters from 5:30 - 7:30 p.m. - free to attend.

news.summary: In Chattanooga, Tennessee, Unum Group (NYSE: UNM) has named Shelia Anderson as executive vice president and chief information and digital officer. Anderson will oversee the digital transformation team, focusing on creating technology solutions to enhance growth and customer experience. Reporting to Unum Group's president and CEO, Rick McKenney, Anderson will drive innovation within the organization.
news.headline: Unum Group appoints Shelia Anderson as EVP, Chief Information & Digital Officer; will lead digital transformation.

news.summary: Research conducted by Bayer U.S. in Whippany, NJ, has revealed valuable insights into the diversity of menopause care approaches and experiences of menopause symptoms. The surveys conducted shed light on the different strategies adopted for managing menopause issues and the wide range of symptoms women encounter during this phase of life. These surveys aim to provide a comprehensive understanding of menopause care practices and highlight the significance of addressing the unique needs of women going through menopause.
news.headline: Bayer U.S. surveys explore menopause care approaches and symptoms diversity.

news.summary: In Oslo, Norway, on May 8, 2025, DNV, an independent assurance and risk management provider, has received approval from the European Commission to serve as a notified body for certifying devices according to the European Union's In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (IVDR). This designation signifies DNV's authority to assess and approve medical devices, meeting the regulatory standards set by the EU. This news underscores DNV's expertise in this field and its compliance with stringent IVDR requirements, marking a significant milestone in the company's operations.
news.headline: DNV recognized as Notified Body under EU's IVDR for certifying devices.Important for regulatory compliance.
Brewers confident of growth despite tariffs threat to sales

news.summary: In Palmetto, Florida, on May 8, 2025, Monster Jam® revealed that the Monster Jam Channel can now be accessed on Prime Video as a no-cost ad-supported (FAST) channel. This move gives families and fans worldwide the opportunity to enjoy the thrilling and unpredictable motorsports entertainment that Monster Jam® has to offer.
news.headline: Feld Entertainment launches Monster Jam Fast Channel on Prime Video, offering free ad-supported content to families and fans.

news.summary: Paycom Software, Inc. exceeded expectations in its first quarter report, with earnings per share coming in at $2.80, surpassing the estimated $2.56 per share. The company also reported sales of $530.50 million for the quarter, higher than the anticipated $524.95 million. Paycom's CEO, Chad Richison, attributed their success to a unique automation approach, effective sales strategies, and operational efficiencies. Richison stated that investments in automation, client returns on investment, and high-quality service are yielding positive results for the company.
news.headline: Paycom Software (PAYC) beats Q1 earnings, with $2.80 EPS vs $2.56 estimate; Sales reach $530.50M vs $524.95M estimate. CEO credits automation for success.

news.summary: Solana's (SOL) price is up 5.66% on Thursday reaching $155.02, representing a 40.1% increase over the last month. The surge is driven by growing institutional interest and efforts to tokenize real-world assets, expanding the network's presence in traditional finance. The rally was sparked by Superstate's announcement of their new platform, "Opening Bell," which will enable SEC-registered public equities to be issued and traded on blockchains, starting with Solana. This move is significant for decentralized finance (DeFi) and regulatory acceptance. The news is detailed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Solana (SOL) surges to $155.02, up 5.66%, with a 40.1% gain in the past 30 days. Superstate announces "Opening Bell" platform for SEC-approved equities on Solana blockchain. #DeFi advancing.

news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) is rapidly approaching the $100,000 milestone once more, with experts predicting a potential surge to $112,000 and beyond. Altcoins, however, are struggling due to Bitcoin's increasing dominance and major market shifts favoring BTC. Currently, Bitcoin is trading at $99,350, with a 24-hour increase of 2.5% and a peak of $99,868. Other cryptocurrencies are also experiencing positive movement, with Ethereum (ETH) rising by 7.5% to $1,965, Solana (SOL) climbing 5.4%, BNB up 1%, and XRP increasing by 4%. Despite this, there is a noticeable disparity between Bitcoin and altcoins. Analysts like Nic Puckrin believe that Bitcoin's current progress is more influenced by geopolitical factors rather than monetary policies. Geopolitical developments seem to be the driving force behind Bitcoin's recent market momentum.
news.headline: Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP surge as BTC predicted to reach $112,000. BTC at $99,350, ETH climbing 7.5% to $1,965, XRP up 4%.

news.summary: Bitcoin surpassed the $100,000 mark on Thursday, hitting $99,469 before slightly dropping, still showing a 2.6% increase for the day and 3.06% for the week. This rally represents a 24.2% rise over the last month, indicating a surge in investor faith due to significant geopolitical and monetary changes. The markets reacted positively to U.S. President Donald Trump's social media declaration of an impending "major trade deal" with the U.K., fostering hope for a potential relaxation of the U.S. trade conflict. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is set to engage in discussions with Chinese representatives in Switzerland, marking the first direct trade negotiations since the recent tariff actions by the Trump administration. Trade crypto futures on Plus500 with bonuses of up to $200, offering speculation on prices without the need for wallets, along with free paper trading for skill enhancement.
news.headline: Bitcoin reaches $100,000 on bullish sentiment, fueled by Trump trade talks and Fed policy. Up 2.6% on the day.
